title: Top-K and Top-P Sampling
concept: Top-K limits sampling to the K most likely words; Top-P (nucleus sampling) limits sampling to a dynamic subset of words whose summed probability reaches P.

exercises.prompt: Why do we use Top-K and Top-P sampling parameters?

options.text: They translate the output into different languages.
They limit the pool of words the AI can choose from for the next word.
They make the AI generate text faster.

exercises.explain: Top-K and Top-P are parameters that restrict which words the AI can select next, preventing it from choosing highly unlikely or bizarre words.

cards.title: Controlling AI Creativity
cards.body:
Have you ever noticed that AI sometimes repeats the exact same boring words, or suddenly says something completely bizarre? 

By tweaking two settings called Top-K and Top-P, we can control the balance between safe, predictable answers and wild, creative ideas!

cards.title: What are Top-K and Top-P?
cards.body:
Top-K and Top-P are settings used to control how an AI chooses the next word:

• **Top-K**: The AI looks at the list of possible next words and only selects from the 'K' most likely words (e.g., if K=10, it only considers the top 10 options).
• **Top-P (Nucleus Sampling)**: The AI looks at the most likely words and adds their probabilities together. It stops adding words once the total probability reaches 'P' (e.g., 90%). It then only chooses from this dynamic group.

cards.title: The Ice Cream Shop Choice
cards.body:
Imagine you are at an ice cream parlor with 50 flavors. 

Top-K sampling is like deciding: 'I will only choose from my top 3 favorite flavors.' No matter how tasty the others look, you restrict your choice to a fixed number of options.

Top-P sampling is like saying: 'I will look at the most popular flavors until I find a group that 90% of customers order.' If chocolate and vanilla make up 90% of sales, you only choose between those two. If it takes 15 different flavors to add up to 90%, you choose from all 15!

cards.title: Top-K vs. Top-P in Action
cards.body:
Let's say the AI is finishing the sentence: "For breakfast, I ate a..."

• With **Top-K = 3**: The AI will ONLY choose between the top 3 options: "banana" (40%), "pancake" (30%), or "bagel" (15%). It ignores everything else.

• With **Top-P = 0.80 (80%)**: The AI adds up the probabilities: "banana" (40%) + "pancake" (30%) + "bagel" (15%) = 85%. Since this exceeds 80%, it stops and chooses only from these three. If the probabilities were flatter, it might have included 10 different words to reach 80%.

cards.title: The 100% Trap
cards.body:
Myth: "Setting Top-P to 1.0 makes the AI completely perfect and error-free."

Fact: Setting Top-P to 1.0 (or 100%) means the AI considers *every single word* in its vocabulary, even the highly unlikely ones. This actually increases the chance of the AI outputting random nonsense or weird typos!

cards.title: Visualizing the Cut-off
cards.body: Let's compare how Top-K and Top-P handle a list of potential next words.

left.text: Looks at the top 'K' words. If K=3, it only chooses from the 3 most likely words, even if the 4th word is also very common.

right.text: Looks at the top words until their summed probability hits 'P' (like 90%). The pool shrinks or grows depending on how likely the words are.

options.text: It restricts the choice to a fixed number of the most likely words.
It only selects words that start with the letter K.
It dynamically expands the word pool based on percentage.
It ensures the AI never repeats the same word twice.

exercises.explain: Top-K restricts the AI to choosing from a fixed, set number of the most likely next words, regardless of their actual probability percentages.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Raising the Top-P value close to 1.0 makes the AI's output more predictable and less creative.
exercises.explain: Setting Top-P to a lower value (like 0.1) restricts the choices to only the most highly probable words, making the output very safe and predictable. Raising Top-P allows more creative and less common words.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how Top-P selects words.
exercises.sentence: Unlike the fixed word count of Top-K, Top-P sampling uses a ____________ selection pool based on cumulative probability.
exercises.explain: Top-P (nucleus) sampling is dynamic because the number of word choices expands or shrinks depending on how confident the AI is about the next word.

exercises.prompt: Which statement correctly identifies a key difference between Top-K and Top-P sampling?

options.text: Top-P only chooses nouns, while Top-K only chooses verbs.
Top-P dynamically changes the number of word choices, while Top-K uses a fixed number.
Top-P is much slower and rarely used compared to Top-K.

exercises.explain: Top-P dynamically adjusts the size of the word pool based on probability, while Top-K always uses a rigid, predetermined number of words.

exercises.prompt: Let's test your mastery! What is the primary difference between Top-K and Top-P sampling, and how does lowering Top-P affect the output?

options.text: Top-K uses a percentage threshold, while Top-P uses a fixed count.
Top-K limits choices to a fixed number, while Top-P dynamically limits choices based on cumulative probability.
Setting Top-P to 0.1 will make the text highly creative, random, and unpredictable.
Both methods force the AI to only choose the single most probable word.

exercises.explain: Top-K limits choices to a fixed number (K), while Top-P dynamically adjusts the number of choices based on their combined probability (P). Lowering P to 0.1 makes the AI choose only from the absolute top-tier, highly likely words, resulting in extremely predictable text.
